title: Tragacantha caespitosa
taxonomy: Tragacantha caespitosa is a member of the Fabaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of legume plants. This family is commonly known as the pea family or legume family. The genus Tragacantha belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which is a subfamily of legumes that includes over 18,000 species. Tragacantha caespitosa is a type of thorny or spiny plant, which is characteristic of many plants in the Fabaceae family. The classification of Tragacantha caespitosa into its specific rank is as follows: genus – Tragacantha, species – T. caespitosa.
uses: Tragacantha caespitosa, also known as the thorny broom, is a plant with potential medicinal properties. Its roots and stems have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments. Tragacantha caespitosa is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
